title: SigmaSeparationSymmetric

tex_statement.html: Note that $\sigma$-separation is symmetric: $A \sPerp_G B \given C \iff B \sPerp_G A \given C$, since when $J = \emptyset$ the set of walks between $A$ and $B$ is the same regardless of direction, and the $\sigma$-blocking conditions are invariant under walk reversal.

tex_proof.html:
Note ŌĆö SigmaSeparationSymmetric

Note that $\sigma$-separation is symmetric: $A \sPerp_G B \given C \iff B \sPerp_G A \given C$, since when $J = \emptyset$ the set of walks between $A$ and $B$ is the same regardless of direction, and the $\sigma$-blocking conditions are invariant under walk reversal.

Proof.

We expand the LN's two-clause sketch into a proof in three steps: (i) unfold $\sPerp_G$ via def_3_18 clause (4); (ii) discharge the $J$-summand on the target side using $J = \emptyset$; (iii) close the resulting equivalence using walk reversal, with the per-walk reversal-invariance of $\sigma$-blocking isolated as a sub-lemma.

Step 1 (unfold $\sPerp_G$). By def_3_18 clause (4), $\sPerp_G$ is the LN's rename of $\isPerp_G$ for the case $J = \emptyset$: $A \sPerp_G B \given C \iff A \isPerp_G B \given C$ and $B \sPerp_G A \given C \iff B \isPerp_G A \given C$. It therefore suffices to prove $A \isPerp_G B \given C \iff B \isPerp_G A \given C$ under the hypothesis $J = \emptyset$.

Step 2 (discharge $J$). Unfolding $\isPerp_G$ via def_3_18 clause (1) and using $J = \emptyset$, so $J \cup B = B$ and $J \cup A = A$, the two sides become:

  • $A \isPerp_G B \given C$ says every walk $\pi$ from a node in $A$ to a node in $B$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked;
  • $B \isPerp_G A \given C$ says every walk $\pi$ from a node in $B$ to a node in $A$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked.

Every walk $\pi = \lp v_0 \sus \cdots \sus v_n \rp$ in $G$ has a reverse $\pi^{-1} := \lp v_n \sus \cdots \sus v_0 \rp$ (the same vertices in reverse order, each incident edge of $G$ traversed in the opposite direction); the map $\pi \mapsto \pi^{-1}$ is an involution, and restricts to a bijection between walks $A \to B$ and walks $B \to A$. The two universals therefore coincide once we know $C$-$\sigma$-blocking is preserved under this involution, which is the content of the following sub-lemma.

Lemma ($\sigma$-blocking is walk-reversal-invariant).\; Let $G$ be a CDMG, $C \ins J \cup V$, and $\pi$ a walk in $G$. Then $\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked iff $\pi^{-1}$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked.

Proof of the lemma. Write $\pi = \lp v_0 \sus \cdots \sus v_n \rp$ and $\pi^{-1} = \lp v_n \sus \cdots \sus v_0 \rp$. Position $k$ on $\pi$ and position $n-k$ on $\pi^{-1}$ refer to the same underlying vertex $v_k$, and the involution $k \leftrightarrow n-k$ is a bijection between positions on $\pi$ and positions on $\pi^{-1}$. We show that all three position-level predicates of def_3_15 and def_3_16 ŌĆō ŌĆ£colliderŌĆØ, ŌĆ£blockable non-colliderŌĆØ, ŌĆ£unblockable non-colliderŌĆØ ŌĆō transport along this involution. Since $\Anc^G(C)$ depends only on $G$ and $C$ (not on any walk), the two existential clauses of def_3_17 clause (2) ŌĆō ŌĆ£exists a collider $v_k$ on $\pi$ with $v_k \notin \Anc^G(C)$ŌĆØ or ŌĆ£exists a blockable non-collider $v_k$ on $\pi$ with $v_k \in C$ŌĆØ ŌĆō are then transported point-wise via $k \leftrightarrow n-k$, and their disjunction (which by def_3_17 clause (2) is the definition of $C$-$\sigma$-blocked) is preserved.

The two edges of $G$ incident to $v_k$ on the walk are physically the same edges on $\pi$ and on $\pi^{-1}$; reversal only swaps which is called the ŌĆ£leftŌĆØ edge (between $v_{k-1}$ and $v_k$) and which the ŌĆ£rightŌĆØ edge (between $v_k$ and $v_{k+1}$) of position $k$, so that the left edge at $k$ on $\pi$ becomes the right edge at $n-k$ on $\pi^{-1}$, and vice versa. The status of each endpoint of each such edge ŌĆō arrowhead, tail, or bidirected tip ŌĆō is a property of the edge in $G$ (recorded by the directed-edge set $E$ or the bidirected-edge set $L$), independent of the walk's direction of traversal. The local triple $(v_{k-1}, v_k, v_{k+1})$ on $\pi$ therefore becomes $(v_{k+1}, v_k, v_{k-1})$ at position $n-k$ on $\pi^{-1}$: the neighbours swap, but $v_k$ and the two incident edges' endpoint statuses are preserved. Consequently:

  • the collider pattern $v_{k-1} \suh v_k \hus v_{k+1}$ (def_3_15 clause (2): two arrowheads at $v_k$) becomes $v_{k+1} \suh v_k \hus v_{k-1}$ at position $n-k$ on $\pi^{-1}$, which is still the collider pattern; so ŌĆ£collider at $k$ on $\pi$ŌĆØ $\iff$ ŌĆ£collider at $n-k$ on $\pi^{-1}$ŌĆØ;
  • the three interior non-collider sub-patterns of def_3_15 clause (1) are exchanged under reversal as left chain $v_{k-1} \hut v_k \hus v_{k+1}$ $\leftrightarrow$ right chain $v_{k+1} \suh v_k \tuh v_{k-1}$ (each becomes the other with the two neighbours' roles swapped), and fork $v_{k-1} \hut v_k \tuh v_{k+1}$ $\leftrightarrow$ fork $v_{k+1} \hut v_k \tuh v_{k-1}$; the end-node sub-pattern $k \in \lC 0,n\rC$ on $\pi$ corresponds to $n-k \in \lC 0,n\rC$ on $\pi^{-1}$ (via $0 \leftrightarrow n$); so ŌĆ£non-collider at $k$ on $\pi$ŌĆØ $\iff$ ŌĆ£non-collider at $n-k$ on $\pi^{-1}$ŌĆØ;
  • the unblockability condition of def_3_16 ŌĆō $k \notin \lC 0,n\rC$ and every outgoing arrow $v_k \tuh v_{k\pm1}$ on the walk lands in $\Sc^G(v_k)$ ŌĆō is preserved: the endpoint condition transports as in the previous bullet, the set of outgoing arrows from $v_k$ on the walk is intrinsic to the two incident edges (the ŌĆ£tail at $v_k$ŌĆØ status of each endpoint is a property of the edge in $G$, not of the walk's direction), and $\Sc^G(v_k)$ depends on $G$ and $v_k$ alone. So ŌĆ£unblockable non-collider at $k$ on $\pi$ŌĆØ $\iff$ ŌĆ£unblockable non-collider at $n-k$ on $\pi^{-1}$ŌĆØ, with the three sub-pattern sides exchanged exactly as in the non-collider bullet.

By the ŌĆ£otherwiseŌĆØ clause of def_3_16, ŌĆ£blockable non-collider on $\pi$ at $k$ŌĆØ is ŌĆ£non-collider on $\pi$ at $k$ and not unblockable non-collider on $\pi$ at $k$ŌĆØ; combining the second and third bullets, this predicate too transports under $k \leftrightarrow n-k$. This establishes the three position-level invariances, and the lemma follows.\hfill$\square$

Step 3 (close the iff). Suppose $A \isPerp_G B \given C$, and let $\pi$ be any walk from a node $b \in B$ to a node $a \in A$. Then $\pi^{-1}$ is a walk from $a \in A$ to $b \in B$, hence $C$-$\sigma$-blocked by hypothesis, hence (by the lemma applied to $\pi^{-1}$, noting $(\pi^{-1})^{-1} = \pi$) $\pi$ is itself $C$-$\sigma$-blocked. This gives $B \isPerp_G A \given C$. The converse implication is identical with the roles of $A$ and $B$ exchanged. Combining with Step 1, $A \sPerp_G B \given C \iff B \sPerp_G A \given C$, as claimed.

lean_code_with_comments:
/-- claim_3_22 (`SigmaSeparationSymmetric`): under the
side-condition $G.J = \emptyset$, the LN's $\sigma$-separation
relation is symmetric in its first two arguments. The LN
statement is "$A \sPerp_G B \given C \iff B \sPerp_G A \given
C$"; the side-condition $G.J = \emptyset$ is the LN's
"$\sigma$-separation" qualifier from def_3_18 clause 4. The
contrapositive "$A \nsPerp_G B \given C \iff B \nsPerp_G A
\given C$" follows by `Iff.not` (`not_congr`) at the call site
and is not stated separately. -/
theorem isSigmaSeparated_symm (G : CDMG ╬▒) (hJ : G.J = Ōłģ)
    (A B C : Set ╬▒) :
    G.IsSigmaSeparated A B C Ōåö G.IsSigmaSeparated B A C := by
  -- Step 1 (unfold $\sPerp_G$): IsSigmaSeparated is `abbrev`-equal to
  -- IsISigmaSeparated, so the abbrev reduction exposes the
  -- three-nested-universal shape.
  change G.IsISigmaSeparated A B C Ōåö G.IsISigmaSeparated B A C
  unfold IsISigmaSeparated
  -- Step 2 (discharge $J$): use `hJ` to collapse `G.J Ōł¬ B = B` and
  -- `G.J Ōł¬ A = A` on both sides of the `Iff`.
  simp only [hJ, Set.empty_union]
  -- Step 3 (close the iff via walk reversal): map each walk
  -- ŽĆ : Walk G v w to ŽĆ.reverse : Walk G w v; the per-walk
  -- Žā-blocking predicate is invariant under reversal (claim_3_22's
  -- sub-lemma, formalised as `Walk.isSigmaBlocked_reverse_iff`).
  refine Ō¤©?_, ?_Ō¤®
  ┬Ę intro h v w hv hw ŽĆ
    rw [ŌåÉ Walk.isSigmaBlocked_reverse_iff]
    exact h hw hv ŽĆ.reverse
  ┬Ę intro h v w hv hw ŽĆ
    rw [ŌåÉ Walk.isSigmaBlocked_reverse_iff]
    exact h hw hv ŽĆ.reverse

/-- The `IsColliderAt` predicate is invariant under walk reversal,
modulo the involution `k Ōåö ŽĆ.length - k`. This is the TeX proof's
"collider pattern $v_{k-1} \suh v_k \hus v_{k+1}$ becomes
$v_{k+1} \suh v_k \hus v_{k-1}$ at position $n-k$" step. -/
theorem isColliderAt_reverse_iff {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) (k : ŌäĢ) :
    ŽĆ.reverse.IsColliderAt k Ōåö ŽĆ.IsColliderAt (ŽĆ.length - k) := by
  induction ŽĆ generalizing k with
  | nil v => simp [Walk.reverse_nil, Walk.length_nil]
  | cons s p ih =>
    cases p with
    | nil w =>
      -- single-step walk: both sides False for any k
      rw [Walk.reverse_cons, Walk.reverse_nil, Walk.nil_append,
        Walk.length_cons, Walk.length_nil]
      cases k with
      | zero => simp
      | succ k' => cases k' <;> simp
    | cons s' p' =>
      -- ŽĆ = cons s (cons s' p'), length p'.length + 2
      have hlen : (Walk.cons s (Walk.cons s' p')).length = p'.length + 2 := by
        simp [Walk.length_cons]
      have hlenRev : (Walk.cons s (Walk.cons s' p')).reverse.length = p'.length + 2 := by
        rw [Walk.length_reverse, hlen]
      rcases Nat.lt_or_ge k (p'.length + 2) with hk | hk
      ┬Ę rcases Nat.lt_or_ge k (p'.length + 1) with hkb | hkb
        ┬Ę -- 0 Ōēż k Ōēż p'.length: interior of (cons s' p').reverse
          rw [Walk.reverse_cons]
          have hkp : k < (Walk.cons s' p').reverse.length := by
            rw [Walk.length_reverse, Walk.length_cons]; omega
          rw [isColliderAt_append_lt_length _ _ hkp]
          rw [ih k]
          simp only [Walk.length_cons]
          -- Goal: (cons s' p').IsColliderAt (p'.length + 1 - k) Ōåö
          --       (cons s (cons s' p')).IsColliderAt (p'.length + 1 + 1 - k)
          have h1 : p'.length + 1 - k = (p'.length - k) + 1 := by omega
          have h2 : p'.length + 1 + 1 - k = (p'.length - k) + 2 := by omega
          rw [h1, h2, Walk.isColliderAt_cons_cons_succ_succ]
        ┬Ę -- k = p'.length + 1: joint case
          have hk_eq : k = p'.length + 1 := by omega
          subst hk_eq
          rw [Walk.reverse_cons, Walk.reverse_cons]
          rw [append_assoc, Walk.cons_append, Walk.nil_append]
          rw [show (p'.length + 1 : ŌäĢ) = p'.reverse.length + 1 from by
            rw [Walk.length_reverse]]
          rw [isColliderAt_append_cons_cons_one p'.reverse s'.reverse s.reverse _]
          rw [WalkStep.reverse_hasArrowheadAtTarget,
            WalkStep.reverse_hasArrowheadAtSource]
          simp only [Walk.length_cons]
          rw [show p'.length + 1 + 1 - (p'.reverse.length + 1) = 1 from by
            rw [Walk.length_reverse]; omega]
          rw [Walk.isColliderAt_cons_cons_one]
          exact And.comm
      ┬Ę -- k Ōēź p'.length + 2 = length: out of range
        have hLHS : ┬¼ (Walk.cons s (Walk.cons s' p')).reverse.IsColliderAt k :=
          not_isColliderAt_of_length_le _ (by rw [hlenRev]; exact hk)
        have hsub : (Walk.cons s (Walk.cons s' p')).length - k = 0 := by
          rw [hlen]; omega
        rw [hsub, Walk.isColliderAt_cons_cons_zero]
        exact iff_false_intro hLHS

/-- The `IsUnblockableNonColliderAt` predicate is invariant under
walk reversal, modulo the involution `k Ōåö ŽĆ.length - k`. The TeX
proof's "unblockability condition ŌĆ” is preserved: the endpoint
condition transports ŌĆ”, the set of outgoing arrows from $v_k$ on
the walk is intrinsic to the two incident edges ŌĆ”, and
$\Sc^G(v_k)$ depends on $G$ and $v_k$ alone" decomposes (via the
`IsUnblockableJoint` joint condition) exactly into the per-step
`reverse_isUnblockableJoint` lemma in `WalkStep`. -/
theorem isUnblockableNonColliderAt_reverse_iff {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) (k : ŌäĢ) :
    ŽĆ.reverse.IsUnblockableNonColliderAt k Ōåö
      ŽĆ.IsUnblockableNonColliderAt (ŽĆ.length - k) := by
  induction ŽĆ generalizing k with
  | nil v => simp [Walk.reverse_nil, Walk.length_nil]
  | cons s p ih =>
    cases p with
    | nil w =>
      rw [Walk.reverse_cons, Walk.reverse_nil, Walk.nil_append,
        Walk.length_cons, Walk.length_nil]
      cases k with
      | zero => simp
      | succ k' => cases k' <;> simp
    | cons s' p' =>
      have hlen : (Walk.cons s (Walk.cons s' p')).length = p'.length + 2 := by
        simp [Walk.length_cons]
      have hlenRev : (Walk.cons s (Walk.cons s' p')).reverse.length = p'.length + 2 := by
        rw [Walk.length_reverse, hlen]
      rcases Nat.lt_or_ge k (p'.length + 2) with hk | hk
      ┬Ę rcases Nat.lt_or_ge k (p'.length + 1) with hkb | hkb
        ┬Ę -- interior of (cons s' p').reverse
          rw [Walk.reverse_cons]
          have hkp : k < (Walk.cons s' p').reverse.length := by
            rw [Walk.length_reverse, Walk.length_cons]; omega
          rw [isUnblockableNonColliderAt_append_lt_length _ _ hkp]
          rw [ih k]
          simp only [Walk.length_cons]
          have h1 : p'.length + 1 - k = (p'.length - k) + 1 := by omega
          have h2 : p'.length + 1 + 1 - k = (p'.length - k) + 2 := by omega
          rw [h1, h2, Walk.isUnblockableNonColliderAt_cons_cons_succ_succ]
        ┬Ę -- joint case
          have hk_eq : k = p'.length + 1 := by omega
          subst hk_eq
          rw [Walk.reverse_cons, Walk.reverse_cons]
          rw [append_assoc, Walk.cons_append, Walk.nil_append]
          rw [show (p'.length + 1 : ŌäĢ) = p'.reverse.length + 1 from by
            rw [Walk.length_reverse]]
          rw [isUnblockableNonColliderAt_append_cons_cons_one p'.reverse
            s'.reverse s.reverse _]
          rw [WalkStep.reverse_isUnblockableJoint]
          simp only [Walk.length_cons]
          rw [show p'.length + 1 + 1 - (p'.reverse.length + 1) = 1 from by
            rw [Walk.length_reverse]; omega]
          rw [Walk.isUnblockableNonColliderAt_cons_cons_one]
      ┬Ę -- k Ōēź length: out of range
        have hLHS : ┬¼ (Walk.cons s (Walk.cons s' p')).reverse.IsUnblockableNonColliderAt k :=
          not_isUnblockableNonColliderAt_of_length_le _
            (by rw [hlenRev]; exact hk)
        have hsub : (Walk.cons s (Walk.cons s' p')).length - k = 0 := by
          rw [hlen]; omega
        rw [hsub, Walk.isUnblockableNonColliderAt_cons_cons_zero]
        exact iff_false_intro hLHS

/-- The `IsNonColliderAt` predicate is invariant under walk
reversal, modulo `k Ōåö ŽĆ.length - k`. Requires `k Ōēż ŽĆ.length`
since `IsNonColliderAt` has a `k Ōēż length` conjunct that would
otherwise spuriously transport. -/
theorem isNonColliderAt_reverse_iff {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) {k : ŌäĢ}
    (hk : k Ōēż ŽĆ.length) :
    ŽĆ.reverse.IsNonColliderAt k Ōåö ŽĆ.IsNonColliderAt (ŽĆ.length - k) := by
  unfold Walk.IsNonColliderAt
  rw [Walk.length_reverse, isColliderAt_reverse_iff]
  refine Ō¤©fun Ō¤©_, hŌ¤® => Ō¤©by omega, hŌ¤®, fun Ō¤©_, hŌ¤® => Ō¤©hk, hŌ¤®Ō¤®

/-- The `IsBlockableNonColliderAt` predicate is invariant under
walk reversal, modulo `k Ōåö ŽĆ.length - k`. The TeX proof's
"combining the second and third bullets, this predicate too
transports under $k \leftrightarrow n-k$" step. -/
theorem isBlockableNonColliderAt_reverse_iff {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) {k : ŌäĢ}
    (hk : k Ōēż ŽĆ.length) :
    ŽĆ.reverse.IsBlockableNonColliderAt k Ōåö
      ŽĆ.IsBlockableNonColliderAt (ŽĆ.length - k) := by
  unfold Walk.IsBlockableNonColliderAt
  rw [isNonColliderAt_reverse_iff ŽĆ hk,
    isUnblockableNonColliderAt_reverse_iff ŽĆ k]

/-- Žā-blocking is invariant under walk reversal: `ŽĆ.IsSigmaBlocked C
Ōåö ŽĆ.reverse.IsSigmaBlocked C`. This is the sub-lemma of the TeX
proof's Step 3 that drives the symmetry of Žā-separation. Each of
the two `IsSigmaBlocked` clauses transports via the involution
`k Ōåö ŽĆ.length - k`, using `isColliderAt_reverse_iff` (resp.
`isBlockableNonColliderAt_reverse_iff`) for the predicate and
`nodeAt_reverse` for the vertex; `G.AncSet C` is intrinsic to
`G` and `C` and so unchanged. -/
theorem isSigmaBlocked_reverse_iff {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) (C : Set ╬▒) :
    ŽĆ.reverse.IsSigmaBlocked C Ōåö ŽĆ.IsSigmaBlocked C := by
  -- Forward and backward use the same construction: extract witness, map index k Ōåö length - k.
  constructor
  ┬Ę rintro (Ō¤©k, hcoll, hOutŌ¤® | Ō¤©k, hblock, hInŌ¤®)
    ┬Ę -- collider witness k on ŽĆ.reverse
      have hk : k < ŽĆ.reverse.length := isColliderAt_lt_length _ hcoll
      rw [Walk.length_reverse] at hk
      have hkLe : k Ōēż ŽĆ.length := le_of_lt hk
      refine Or.inl Ō¤©ŽĆ.length - k, ?_, ?_Ō¤®
      ┬Ę rw [isColliderAt_reverse_iff] at hcoll; exact hcoll
      ┬Ę rw [nodeAt_reverse ŽĆ hkLe] at hOut; exact hOut
    ┬Ę -- blockable non-collider witness k on ŽĆ.reverse
      have hkRevLe : k Ōēż ŽĆ.reverse.length := hblock.1.1
      have hkLe : k Ōēż ŽĆ.length := by
        rw [Walk.length_reverse] at hkRevLe; exact hkRevLe
      refine Or.inr Ō¤©ŽĆ.length - k, ?_, ?_Ō¤®
      ┬Ę rw [isBlockableNonColliderAt_reverse_iff ŽĆ hkLe] at hblock; exact hblock
      ┬Ę rw [nodeAt_reverse ŽĆ hkLe] at hIn; exact hIn
  ┬Ę rintro (Ō¤©k, hcoll, hOutŌ¤® | Ō¤©k, hblock, hInŌ¤®)
    ┬Ę -- collider witness k on ŽĆ
      have hk : k < ŽĆ.length := isColliderAt_lt_length _ hcoll
      have hkLe : k Ōēż ŽĆ.length := le_of_lt hk
      have hkSub : ŽĆ.length - k Ōēż ŽĆ.length := Nat.sub_le _ _
      refine Or.inl Ō¤©ŽĆ.length - k, ?_, ?_Ō¤®
      ┬Ę rw [isColliderAt_reverse_iff]
        rw [show ŽĆ.length - (ŽĆ.length - k) = k from by omega]
        exact hcoll
      ┬Ę rw [nodeAt_reverse ŽĆ hkSub]
        rw [show ŽĆ.length - (ŽĆ.length - k) = k from by omega]
        exact hOut
    ┬Ę -- blockable non-collider witness k on ŽĆ
      have hkLe : k Ōēż ŽĆ.length := hblock.1.1
      have hkSub : ŽĆ.length - k Ōēż ŽĆ.length := Nat.sub_le _ _
      refine Or.inr Ō¤©ŽĆ.length - k, ?_, ?_Ō¤®
      ┬Ę rw [isBlockableNonColliderAt_reverse_iff ŽĆ hkSub]
        rw [show ŽĆ.length - (ŽĆ.length - k) = k from by omega]
        exact hblock
      ┬Ę rw [nodeAt_reverse ŽĆ hkSub]
        rw [show ŽĆ.length - (ŽĆ.length - k) = k from by omega]
        exact hIn
lean_code_without_comments:
theorem isSigmaSeparated_symm (G : CDMG ╬▒) (hJ : G.J = Ōłģ)
    (A B C : Set ╬▒) :
    G.IsSigmaSeparated A B C Ōåö G.IsSigmaSeparated B A C := by

  change G.IsISigmaSeparated A B C Ōåö G.IsISigmaSeparated B A C
  unfold IsISigmaSeparated

  simp only [hJ, Set.empty_union]

  refine Ō¤©?_, ?_Ō¤®
  ┬Ę intro h v w hv hw ŽĆ
    rw [ŌåÉ Walk.isSigmaBlocked_reverse_iff]
    exact h hw hv ŽĆ.reverse
  ┬Ę intro h v w hv hw ŽĆ
    rw [ŌåÉ Walk.isSigmaBlocked_reverse_iff]
    exact h hw hv ŽĆ.reverse

theorem isColliderAt_reverse_iff {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) (k : ŌäĢ) :
    ŽĆ.reverse.IsColliderAt k Ōåö ŽĆ.IsColliderAt (ŽĆ.length - k) := by
  induction ŽĆ generalizing k with
  | nil v => simp [Walk.reverse_nil, Walk.length_nil]
  | cons s p ih =>
    cases p with
    | nil w =>

      rw [Walk.reverse_cons, Walk.reverse_nil, Walk.nil_append,
        Walk.length_cons, Walk.length_nil]
      cases k with
      | zero => simp
      | succ k' => cases k' <;> simp
    | cons s' p' =>

      have hlen : (Walk.cons s (Walk.cons s' p')).length = p'.length + 2 := by
        simp [Walk.length_cons]
      have hlenRev : (Walk.cons s (Walk.cons s' p')).reverse.length = p'.length + 2 := by
        rw [Walk.length_reverse, hlen]
      rcases Nat.lt_or_ge k (p'.length + 2) with hk | hk
      ┬Ę rcases Nat.lt_or_ge k (p'.length + 1) with hkb | hkb
        ┬Ę
          rw [Walk.reverse_cons]
          have hkp : k < (Walk.cons s' p').reverse.length := by
            rw [Walk.length_reverse, Walk.length_cons]; omega
          rw [isColliderAt_append_lt_length _ _ hkp]
          rw [ih k]
          simp only [Walk.length_cons]

          have h1 : p'.length + 1 - k = (p'.length - k) + 1 := by omega
          have h2 : p'.length + 1 + 1 - k = (p'.length - k) + 2 := by omega
          rw [h1, h2, Walk.isColliderAt_cons_cons_succ_succ]
        ┬Ę
          have hk_eq : k = p'.length + 1 := by omega
          subst hk_eq
          rw [Walk.reverse_cons, Walk.reverse_cons]
          rw [append_assoc, Walk.cons_append, Walk.nil_append]
          rw [show (p'.length + 1 : ŌäĢ) = p'.reverse.length + 1 from by
            rw [Walk.length_reverse]]
          rw [isColliderAt_append_cons_cons_one p'.reverse s'.reverse s.reverse _]
          rw [WalkStep.reverse_hasArrowheadAtTarget,
            WalkStep.reverse_hasArrowheadAtSource]
          simp only [Walk.length_cons]
          rw [show p'.length + 1 + 1 - (p'.reverse.length + 1) = 1 from by
            rw [Walk.length_reverse]; omega]
          rw [Walk.isColliderAt_cons_cons_one]
          exact And.comm
      ┬Ę
        have hLHS : ┬¼ (Walk.cons s (Walk.cons s' p')).reverse.IsColliderAt k :=
          not_isColliderAt_of_length_le _ (by rw [hlenRev]; exact hk)
        have hsub : (Walk.cons s (Walk.cons s' p')).length - k = 0 := by
          rw [hlen]; omega
        rw [hsub, Walk.isColliderAt_cons_cons_zero]
        exact iff_false_intro hLHS

theorem isUnblockableNonColliderAt_reverse_iff {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) (k : ŌäĢ) :
    ŽĆ.reverse.IsUnblockableNonColliderAt k Ōåö
      ŽĆ.IsUnblockableNonColliderAt (ŽĆ.length - k) := by
  induction ŽĆ generalizing k with
  | nil v => simp [Walk.reverse_nil, Walk.length_nil]
  | cons s p ih =>
    cases p with
    | nil w =>
      rw [Walk.reverse_cons, Walk.reverse_nil, Walk.nil_append,
        Walk.length_cons, Walk.length_nil]
      cases k with
      | zero => simp
      | succ k' => cases k' <;> simp
    | cons s' p' =>
      have hlen : (Walk.cons s (Walk.cons s' p')).length = p'.length + 2 := by
        simp [Walk.length_cons]
      have hlenRev : (Walk.cons s (Walk.cons s' p')).reverse.length = p'.length + 2 := by
        rw [Walk.length_reverse, hlen]
      rcases Nat.lt_or_ge k (p'.length + 2) with hk | hk
      ┬Ę rcases Nat.lt_or_ge k (p'.length + 1) with hkb | hkb
        ┬Ę
          rw [Walk.reverse_cons]
          have hkp : k < (Walk.cons s' p').reverse.length := by
            rw [Walk.length_reverse, Walk.length_cons]; omega
          rw [isUnblockableNonColliderAt_append_lt_length _ _ hkp]
          rw [ih k]
          simp only [Walk.length_cons]
          have h1 : p'.length + 1 - k = (p'.length - k) + 1 := by omega
          have h2 : p'.length + 1 + 1 - k = (p'.length - k) + 2 := by omega
          rw [h1, h2, Walk.isUnblockableNonColliderAt_cons_cons_succ_succ]
        ┬Ę
          have hk_eq : k = p'.length + 1 := by omega
          subst hk_eq
          rw [Walk.reverse_cons, Walk.reverse_cons]
          rw [append_assoc, Walk.cons_append, Walk.nil_append]
          rw [show (p'.length + 1 : ŌäĢ) = p'.reverse.length + 1 from by
            rw [Walk.length_reverse]]
          rw [isUnblockableNonColliderAt_append_cons_cons_one p'.reverse
            s'.reverse s.reverse _]
          rw [WalkStep.reverse_isUnblockableJoint]
          simp only [Walk.length_cons]
          rw [show p'.length + 1 + 1 - (p'.reverse.length + 1) = 1 from by
            rw [Walk.length_reverse]; omega]
          rw [Walk.isUnblockableNonColliderAt_cons_cons_one]
      ┬Ę
        have hLHS : ┬¼ (Walk.cons s (Walk.cons s' p')).reverse.IsUnblockableNonColliderAt k :=
          not_isUnblockableNonColliderAt_of_length_le _
            (by rw [hlenRev]; exact hk)
        have hsub : (Walk.cons s (Walk.cons s' p')).length - k = 0 := by
          rw [hlen]; omega
        rw [hsub, Walk.isUnblockableNonColliderAt_cons_cons_zero]
        exact iff_false_intro hLHS

theorem isNonColliderAt_reverse_iff {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) {k : ŌäĢ}
    (hk : k Ōēż ŽĆ.length) :
    ŽĆ.reverse.IsNonColliderAt k Ōåö ŽĆ.IsNonColliderAt (ŽĆ.length - k) := by
  unfold Walk.IsNonColliderAt
  rw [Walk.length_reverse, isColliderAt_reverse_iff]
  refine Ō¤©fun Ō¤©_, hŌ¤® => Ō¤©by omega, hŌ¤®, fun Ō¤©_, hŌ¤® => Ō¤©hk, hŌ¤®Ō¤®

theorem isBlockableNonColliderAt_reverse_iff {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) {k : ŌäĢ}
    (hk : k Ōēż ŽĆ.length) :
    ŽĆ.reverse.IsBlockableNonColliderAt k Ōåö
      ŽĆ.IsBlockableNonColliderAt (ŽĆ.length - k) := by
  unfold Walk.IsBlockableNonColliderAt
  rw [isNonColliderAt_reverse_iff ŽĆ hk,
    isUnblockableNonColliderAt_reverse_iff ŽĆ k]

theorem isSigmaBlocked_reverse_iff {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) (C : Set ╬▒) :
    ŽĆ.reverse.IsSigmaBlocked C Ōåö ŽĆ.IsSigmaBlocked C := by

  constructor
  ┬Ę rintro (Ō¤©k, hcoll, hOutŌ¤® | Ō¤©k, hblock, hInŌ¤®)
    ┬Ę
      have hk : k < ŽĆ.reverse.length := isColliderAt_lt_length _ hcoll
      rw [Walk.length_reverse] at hk
      have hkLe : k Ōēż ŽĆ.length := le_of_lt hk
      refine Or.inl Ō¤©ŽĆ.length - k, ?_, ?_Ō¤®
      ┬Ę rw [isColliderAt_reverse_iff] at hcoll; exact hcoll
      ┬Ę rw [nodeAt_reverse ŽĆ hkLe] at hOut; exact hOut
    ┬Ę
      have hkRevLe : k Ōēż ŽĆ.reverse.length := hblock.1.1
      have hkLe : k Ōēż ŽĆ.length := by
        rw [Walk.length_reverse] at hkRevLe; exact hkRevLe
      refine Or.inr Ō¤©ŽĆ.length - k, ?_, ?_Ō¤®
      ┬Ę rw [isBlockableNonColliderAt_reverse_iff ŽĆ hkLe] at hblock; exact hblock
      ┬Ę rw [nodeAt_reverse ŽĆ hkLe] at hIn; exact hIn
  ┬Ę rintro (Ō¤©k, hcoll, hOutŌ¤® | Ō¤©k, hblock, hInŌ¤®)
    ┬Ę
      have hk : k < ŽĆ.length := isColliderAt_lt_length _ hcoll
      have hkLe : k Ōēż ŽĆ.length := le_of_lt hk
      have hkSub : ŽĆ.length - k Ōēż ŽĆ.length := Nat.sub_le _ _
      refine Or.inl Ō¤©ŽĆ.length - k, ?_, ?_Ō¤®
      ┬Ę rw [isColliderAt_reverse_iff]
        rw [show ŽĆ.length - (ŽĆ.length - k) = k from by omega]
        exact hcoll
      ┬Ę rw [nodeAt_reverse ŽĆ hkSub]
        rw [show ŽĆ.length - (ŽĆ.length - k) = k from by omega]
        exact hOut
    ┬Ę
      have hkLe : k Ōēż ŽĆ.length := hblock.1.1
      have hkSub : ŽĆ.length - k Ōēż ŽĆ.length := Nat.sub_le _ _
      refine Or.inr Ō¤©ŽĆ.length - k, ?_, ?_Ō¤®
      ┬Ę rw [isBlockableNonColliderAt_reverse_iff ŽĆ hkSub]
        rw [show ŽĆ.length - (ŽĆ.length - k) = k from by omega]
        exact hblock
      ┬Ę rw [nodeAt_reverse ŽĆ hkSub]
        rw [show ŽĆ.length - (ŽĆ.length - k) = k from by omega]
        exact hIn
lean_explanation:
The main theorem `isSigmaSeparated_symm` (in `SigmaSeparationSymmetric.lean`) formalises the LN's claim $A \sPerp_G B \given C \iff B \sPerp_G A \given C$ under the side-condition $J = \emptyset$. Its binders correspond directly to the LN: `╬▒ : Type*` is the implicit ambient vertex carrier, `G : CDMG ╬▒` is the LN's conditional directed mixed graph $G$, the hypothesis `hJ : G.J = Ōłģ` carries the LN's $J = \emptyset$ qualifier (which the LN encodes into its choice of symbol $\sPerp_G$ versus $\isPerp_G$, never as an explicit hypothesis), and `A B C : Set ╬▒` are the LN's vertex/input subsets $A$, $B$, $C$. The expression `G.IsSigmaSeparated A B C` reads as $A \sPerp_G B \given C$, and `Ōåö` is Lean's notation for `Iff` ŌĆö the LN's $\iff$.

`IsSigmaSeparated` is an `abbrev` of `IsISigmaSeparated` (the Lean `def` formalising def_3_18 clause (1)), so the proof opens by `change`-ing the goal to the latter and `unfold`-ing it to expose the three-nested universal ŌĆö for every `v Ōłł G.J Ōł¬ A`, `w Ōłł G.J Ōł¬ B`, and `ŽĆ : Walk G v w`, the walk `ŽĆ` is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked. `simp only [hJ, Set.empty_union]` then collapses `G.J Ōł¬ A` to `A` and `G.J Ōł¬ B` to `B`, and `refine Ō¤©?_, ?_Ō¤®` splits the resulting `Iff` into its two halves. Each half maps a walk $B \to A$ to its reverse via `Walk.reverse` and transports $\sigma$-blocking through the involution using the sub-lemma `Walk.isSigmaBlocked_reverse_iff`.

The walk-reversal infrastructure lives in `SigmaBlockedReversal.lean`. The central sub-lemma `isSigmaBlocked_reverse_iff` states `ŽĆ.IsSigmaBlocked C Ōåö ŽĆ.reverse.IsSigmaBlocked C` and decomposes into three position-level invariances along the involution $k \leftrightarrow n - k$ (where `ŽĆ.length` is the LN's walk length $n$ and `k : ŌäĢ` indexes the LN's vertex $v_k$): `isColliderAt_reverse_iff` for colliders (def_3_15), `isUnblockableNonColliderAt_reverse_iff` for unblockable non-colliders (def_3_16), and ŌĆö derived from these together with `isNonColliderAt_reverse_iff` ŌĆö `isBlockableNonColliderAt_reverse_iff` for the remaining case. Each position-level lemma proceeds by induction on the walk with a case split on whether $k$ lies in the interior of the reversed walk or at its joint position. The joint case relies on per-step reversal facts in `WalkStep` (each edge's arrowhead status is intrinsic to $G$, so reversal swaps which endpoint is the *source* and *target* without changing the underlying data), wrapped up in `WalkStep.reverse_isUnblockableJoint`; bookkeeping helpers (`nodeAt_reverse`, `append_assoc`, `isColliderAt_append_lt_length`, and friends) handle the structural-recursion plumbing.
design_choices:
**Stated on `IsSigmaSeparated`, not on `IsISigmaSeparated`.** The LN phrases the claim on the $\sPerp_G$ surface ŌĆö the $J = \emptyset$ specialisation of $\isPerp_G$ ŌĆö not on the underlying three-nested universal. Because `IsSigmaSeparated` is an `abbrev` of `IsISigmaSeparated`, the proof recovers the universal shape for free via `abbrev`-reduction while the LN notation stays visible in the goal: downstream consumers (CBN-Markov, do-calculus, identification criteria) pattern on $\sPerp_G$, not the unfolded form. Stating symmetry on `IsISigmaSeparated` directly was rejected because it would be both literally false in general (the $J$-on-the-target footnote of def_3_18 clause (1) makes $i\sigma$-separation asymmetric whenever $J \neq \emptyset$) and ergonomically worse ŌĆö every consumer would have to unfold the abbrev before applying the result.

**`hJ : G.J = Ōłģ` is an explicit caller's hypothesis.** The LN encodes the side-condition into the symbol $\sPerp_G$ itself; since the Lean `abbrev` reduces transparently, the explicit `hJ` is the only carrier of the condition. A baked-in alternative ŌĆö a wrapper `structure NoInputCDMG ╬▒ extends CDMG ╬▒ (hJ : J = Ōłģ)` ŌĆö would type-level-enforce the condition at the cost of duplicating every $i\sigma$-separation lemma on the wrapper (or threading coercions everywhere) and would diverge from the LN's "same notion, different name" reading of def_3_18 clause (4). Downstream consumers in chapters 4ŌĆō7 already carry $G.J = \emptyset$ in scope (e.g. the no-input cuts in do-calculus), so threading it explicitly is the natural composition pattern.

**A single `Iff`, no contrapositive twin.** The LN writes a single $\iff$. The contrapositive $A \nsPerp_G B \given C \iff B \nsPerp_G A \given C$ follows in one line at the call site via `not_congr isSigmaSeparated_symm`, since `IsNotSigmaSeparated` is `abbrev`-equal to the negation of `IsISigmaSeparated`. Exposing a second theorem would clutter the API for no LN-side gain.

**Walk-reversal infrastructure isolated in `SigmaBlockedReversal.lean`.** The LN's "$\sigma$-blocking conditions are invariant under walk reversal" is the *justification* for symmetry, not part of the statement. Exposing reversal lemmas in `isSigmaSeparated_symm`'s signature would conflate the *what* with the *why*; instead the four position-level transports (`isColliderAt_reverse_iff`, `isUnblockableNonColliderAt_reverse_iff`, `isNonColliderAt_reverse_iff`, `isBlockableNonColliderAt_reverse_iff`) and the combined sub-lemma `isSigmaBlocked_reverse_iff` live in their own file, mirroring the LN's separation of the walk-reversal sub-lemma from the main proof. The isolation also keeps the structural-recursion bookkeeping (`nodeAt_reverse`, `append_assoc`, `isColliderAt_append_lt_length`, etc.) out of the statement file.

**No subset side-condition on `A, B, C`.** The LN's preamble "$A, B, C \subseteq J \cup V$" is a caller-side hypothesis, not a type-level guard. Symmetry holds for arbitrary `Set ╬▒` ŌĆö parts of $A$ or $B$ outside the node set support no walks at all, so both sides of the `Iff` are vacuously true there ŌĆö and omitting the condition is consistent with the rest of section 3.3.

**No mathlib counterpart.** The graph-theoretic $\sigma$-blocking apparatus is project-local; mathlib's `SimpleGraph` and `Quiver` carry no collider or arrowhead data, so the stack is built from scratch on the bespoke `CDMG` namespace. The only mathlib pieces visible in the main statement are `Set`, `Iff`, and `Ōłģ` ŌĆö standard logical glue ŌĆö while the proof reaches for `Set.empty_union` to discharge the $J$-collapse.
